long_stay: Student exchange programs for secondary education
Professional training/vocational courses (levels 4–5 of the National Qualifications Framework)
Unpaid internships and traineeships linked to a recognized program
Language courses (for programs exceeding 90 days or as part of a broader study plan)
short_stay: Courses of 90 days or less (may require a short-stay Schengen visa, Type C, depending on nationality)

types.name: Long-stay Study Visa
types.notes: Allows two entries and is valid for 120 days, during which the student must apply for a residence permit.

booking_methods.description: Applications begin on the Portuguese Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MNE) E-visa portal.
In many countries, the Portuguese government outsources visa application collection to VFS Global. Applicants must register on the VFS website specific to their country to book a slot.
In jurisdictions where VFS is not available, students must contact the consular section of the Portuguese Embassy directly via official email or telephone.

steps.title: Secure Admission
steps.description: Obtain admission or acceptance from a Portuguese educational institution.

steps.title: Online Application
steps.description: Register and fill out the online application form on the E-visa portal.

steps.title: Schedule and Attend Interview
steps.description: Schedule and attend an interview at the consulate or VFS center to submit documents and provide biometric data (fingerprints).

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 3 months beyond intended stay, with at least two blank pages.

required_documents.name: Proof of Regular Status
required_documents.details: Required if applying from a country other than the country of origin.

required_documents.name: Criminal Record Certificate
required_documents.details: From the country of origin or residence (for over a year). Must be apostilled or legalized.

required_documents.name: Permission for AIMA to Check Portuguese Criminal Records
Valid Travel Medical Insurance
required_documents.details: Valid for the intended stay (minimum 1 year for residence permit), minimum coverage of €30,000, including repatriation, covering the Schengen area.

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: Lease agreement, university residence letter, or 'term of responsibility' from a host.

required_documents.name: Proof of Financial Means
required_documents.details: Bank statements, scholarship grant, or 'term of responsibility'.
exemptions: Students with a 'term of responsibility' signed by a Portuguese citizen/resident
Students with a 'term of responsibility' signed by a Portuguese citizen/resident

residence_permit.authority_full_name: Agency for Integration, Migration, and Asylum
residence_permit.booking_method: Pre-scheduled appointment (on visa sticker), AIMA contact center, or online channel.
required_documents: Health insurance or proof of coverage by the National Health Service (SNS)
residence_permit.notes: The study visa (Type D) allows two entries and is valid for 120 days, during which the student must apply for a residence permit.

work_while_studying.legal_basis: Article 97 of the Foreign Nationals Law (REPSAE)
restrictions: Work must not interfere with the student's study schedule or academic performance.
Secondary school students are generally not authorized to work.
work_while_studying.notes: Portugal's current legal text does not set a specific maximum number of weekly hours for higher-education students; 20 hours/week during term time is an informal limit. Students should confirm any hour limits directly with AIMA or their university.

permit_renewal.online_portal: AIMA online portal (Automatic Renewal) / Portal das Renovações
required_documents: Current residence permit (even if recently expired)
Proof of continued enrollment for the new academic year
Proof of successful completion of the previous year’s subjects (academic transcript)
Updated proof of financial means and accommodation
permit_renewal.notes: Renewal periods generally match the original permit's logic (one-year periods for standard student permits).

requirements: Proof of completed studies (academic degree obtained in Portugal) or research project.
Valid job contract or a job offer, or proof of registering as self-employed with the Tax Authority.
Copy of passport and current residence permit.
post_study_work.notes: Graduates can apply for a residence permit for job searching or for work as an employee or independent professional. This involves a change of status request through AIMA's dedicated contact form, not the standard renewal portal.

official_sources.name: Portal de Vistos (Ministry of Foreign Affairs)
official_sources.description: Information on visa types, fees, processing times, required documentation, and legislation.

official_sources.name: AIMA (Agency for Integration, Migration and Asylum)
official_sources.description: Information on residence permits, renewals, work authorization, and legal texts.

official_sources.name: Study in Portugal (Official Higher Education Portal)
European Commission – EU Immigration Portal, Portugal section
official_sources.description: EU-level overview and conditions to study in Portugal as a non-EU citizen.

application_forms.name: Ministry of Foreign Affairs Forms Page

tips.title: Apostille Documents
tips.body: Ensure your criminal record and academic certificates are apostilled (Hague Convention) or legalized by the Portuguese consulate in your home country before leaving.

tips.title: NIF and Bank Account
tips.body: Apply for a Portuguese Tax Number (NIF) and open a Portuguese bank account as soon as possible, as these are often required for the residence permit.

tips.title: Health Insurance
tips.body: Ensure your insurance is valid for the entire duration of the visa (120 days) and covers the whole Schengen area.

tips.title: Timing
tips.body: Start the application at least 3-4 months before the course start date, as consulate slots fill up quickly.

tips.title: Direct Communication
tips.body: Check the specific requirements of the consulate where you are applying, as small variations in document requirements (like 'term of responsibility' formats) can occur between different regions.

common_mistakes.mistake: Submitting an incomplete or incorrectly filled document set
common_mistakes.consequence: Can result in a rejection notice and a longer overall procedure.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too close to the intended travel or course start date
common_mistakes.consequence: Given the official decision period (up to 60 days) plus appointment wait times, this can lead to delays.

common_mistakes.mistake: Underestimating the proof of means of subsistence, or submitting bank statements that don't clearly show the required reference amount per month
Not booking the AIMA residence permit appointment soon enough after arrival
common_mistakes.consequence: The residence visa's entry window is limited.

common_mistakes.mistake: Starting paid work without first notifying AIMA
common_mistakes.consequence: Can create irregularities affecting future renewals.

common_mistakes.mistake: Not checking whether their specific nationality or course type qualifies for fee exemptions or simplified procedures
